meanings.definition: the act of moving from one place to another
examples: his constant shifting disrupted the class

meanings.definition: make a shift in or exchange of
examples: First Joe led; then we switched
transfer the packet from his trouser pockets to a pocket in his jacket

meanings.definition: move from one setting or context to another

examples: shift the date for our class reunion

meanings.definition: move sideways or in an unsteady way
examples: The ship careened out of control
The ship suddenly lurched to the left

meanings.definition: use a shift key on a keyboard
examples: She could not shift so all her letters are written in lower case

meanings.definition: change phonetically as part of a systematic historical change
examples: Grimm showed how the consonants shifted
you have to shift when you go down a steep hill

meanings.definition: lay aside, abandon, or leave for another
examples: switch to a different brand of beer
he drifted into the shifting crowd
